title: H1-H5 constant ceiling and pressure-to-one exclusion
certified_here: C1: lambda_candidate > 7/24 (exact rational), i.e. this rate would give a constant strictly better than the EHPS 2024 baseline IF a survivor certificate for it existed
C2a: Lambda_cap = (theta_3/3)^2 <= 211/250, certified by the purely rational inequality R^2 <= (9U)^3 with R = (1+x0+x0^2)^3/x0^2 >= theta_3^3
C2b: any family obeying the pressure bracket 1-rho(m) <= 2/(3m)+11/(6m^2) has rho(m) >= 85/98 > 211/250 for every m >= 7, hence exceeds the ceiling and cannot be an H1-H5 survivor
not_certified_here: the constant map c = 2*sqrt(log2(1/lambda)) itself (external, sealed-by-audit, not formalized, not re-derived in this repo)
the cap-set ceiling Lambda_cap on H1-H5 survivor return rates (external, from published Ellenberg-Gijswijt plus a Fubini + periodic Perron-Frobenius argument; sealed-by-audit)
the pressure bracket, which is an external PROVED input for one specific family and is treated here as a hypothesis
any r_3(N) lower bound; no survivor certificate exists

display_only_decimals.note: informational; never in the trust path

consequence: Because c = 2*sqrt(log2(1/lambda)) is strictly decreasing in lambda and no H1-H5 survivor may exceed Lambda_cap, no survivor in this framework can have a constant below ~0.9914. The framework can therefore improve the CONSTANT but can never beat the sqrt(log N) ORDER. Separately, pressure-to-one families are excluded outright: the closer rho is driven to 1, the more decisively the ceiling is violated.
